plan_summary.primary_goal: Design, certify, and distribute a single-SKU Faraday enclosure on a €400K Year-1 budget, hitting €50K net cash from operations by Month 12 to unlock €350K follow-on funding for the MIL-STD server-grade pivot.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Tests five gates: Year-1 net-cash margin against the €50K Month-12 funding trigger; ISO-run sell-through margin against the 1,500-unit pivot threshold; gross-profit margin against the €120K MIL-STD certification cost; Year-1 budget surplus against the €400K ceiling; and validated-demand margin against the 1,000-unit Tallinn ISO commitment threshold. Funder cash-flow trigger renegotiation, EU EMC certification pass, single-source gasket continuity, and European utility-model filing are unmodelled existential gates.

key_values.label: Month-12 funding-trigger net-cash threshold
key_values.comment: €50K net cash from operations by Month 12 (excluding initial €400K investment) is the renegotiated funding trigger; below it the €350K follow-on is withheld.
key_values.source_text: negotiate a specific '€50k net cash from operations by Month 12'.

key_values.label: ISO-run sell-through pivot-funding threshold
key_values.comment: 75% sell-through of the 2,000-unit ISO run = 1,500 units funds the server-grade pivot; below this Risk 2 leaves €75K+ unsold inventory.
key_values.source_text: If pre-sales fall short of 1,500 units within 6 months, then the project is left with 2,000 unsold enclosures.

key_values.label: MIL-STD-461 certification cost for the server-grade pivot
key_values.comment: €120K MIL-STD-461 certification is the spend the Year-1 gross profit must cover to unlock the server-grade pivot; below it the B2B pivot is delayed.
key_values.source_text: If the €99 price yields gross profit less than €120k, then the server-grade pivot certification cannot be funded.

key_values.label: Year-1 burn-commitment ceiling
key_values.comment: Year-1 €400K envelope (€150K production + €80K cert + €100K marketing + €50K ops + €20K contingency); above it the contingency-cut cascade begins.
key_values.source_text: Year-1 budget burn commitment: €400 k — initial cash input for Monte Carlo.

key_values.label: Tallinn-ISO-commitment demand-validation threshold
key_values.comment: Demand confirmed post-Latvian-500-run must exceed 1,000 units to commit to the full 2,000-unit Tallinn ISO run; below it the project stays at 500-unit batches.
key_values.source_text: If demand confirmed post-Latvian run falls below 1,000 units, then commitment to the Tallinn ISO facility is delayed.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved Year-1 net cash from operations at Month 12
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Drives whether the €50K funding trigger clears; Issue 2 names 2-month cert delay reducing revenue by €150K, Risk 1 names single-late-payment killing the trigger.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Year-1 revenue (two-tier pricing × sell-through × wholesale-mix) minus COGS minus opex; sensitivity to certification timing (Issue 2), retailer payment terms (Data Item 4), and chargeback rate (Risk 9).

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved units sold from the 2,000-unit ISO run within 6 months
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Above 1,500 units the pivot is funded; below it Risk 2's €75K unsold-inventory scenario activates and the server-grade pivot stalls.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: European prepper TAM (15K–20K active buyers) × conversion rate (Issue 1 names 2–5%) × wholesale-channel uplift; sensitivity to single-SKU phone/laptop compromise risk.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved Year-1 gross profit across two-tier pricing mix
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Above €120K the MIL-STD-461 server-grade certification is funded; below it the pivot is delayed and the binary-bet failure mode named in Risk 4 activates.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Revenue (units × blended ARPU) × gross-margin mix (45% at €99 vs 55% D2C uplift on €149 enclosure); sensitivity to Risk 4 (€99 → €89K profit gap) and Risk 10 wholesale-discount erosion.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved Year-1 total spend across production, certification, marketing, opex
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Tests whether the €400K Year-1 envelope holds; Issue 2 names 40% cert overrun (+€32K), Risk 6 names 12% material premium for supplier diversification.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Sum production (€150K) + cert (€80K + Issue-2 contingency) + marketing (€100K) + opex (€50K) + Risk-6 material premium + Risk-7 fulfillment-transition cost; sensitivity to Risks 2, 6, 7.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved confirmed demand units after the 500-unit Latvian batch
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Above 1,000 units the Tallinn ISO commitment proceeds; below it the project defaults to smaller batches and delays the B2B pivot.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Pre-order conversion rate × 500-unit-launch reach × repeat-purchase signal + B2B Letter-of-Intent unit-equivalent; sensitivity to Issue 1 conversion-rate variance and Risk 3 phone/laptop SKU compromise.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Year-1 net-cash margin against the €50K funding trigger
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin unlocks the €350K follow-on funding for the server-grade pivot; negative margin kills the entire pivot thesis regardless of operational success.

recommended_first_calculations.label: ISO-run sell-through margin against the 1,500-unit pivot threshold
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin clears inventory and funds the next stage; negative margin triggers Risk 2's €75K unsold-inventory scenario and depletes the cash runway.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Gross-profit margin against the €120K MIL-STD certification cost
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin funds the server-grade pivot certification internally; negative margin forces the binary-bet pricing problem named in Risk 4 (€89K at €99) and stalls the B2B pivot.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Year-1 budget surplus against the €400K Year-1 ceiling
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive surplus preserves the 5% contingency for cert re-test or supplier resilience; negative surplus forces marketing cuts that depress the very pre-sales the pivot trigger depends on.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Validated-demand margin against the 1,000-unit Tallinn ISO commitment threshold
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin justifies committing €150K to the 2,000-unit ISO run; negative margin keeps production at the 500-unit Latvian-batch level and delays the certification-funded pivot.

unmodelled_gates.label: Funder agrees to renegotiate 'positive cash flow' to '€50K net cash by Month 12' by Month 6
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 1 names this as the single biggest governance risk: without renegotiation the funder retains unilateral interpretation of the trigger, and a single late retailer payment can withhold the €350K follow-on regardless of operational success.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: €350K follow-on lost; server-grade pivot defunded; project terminates with €400K sunk Year-1 investment.

unmodelled_gates.label: EU EMC Directive certification (CE mark) achieved before Year-1 retail launch
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 5 / Issue 2 name certification as a hard prerequisite for consumer market entry; Issue 2 specifically names 30–50% cost escalation on test failure and 3-month delay killing the funding trigger.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Consumer retail launch blocked; Year-1 revenue collapses to near-zero; funding trigger unreachable.

unmodelled_gates.label: Conductive gasket / copper-beryllium finger stock supply continuity for the ISO run
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 6 names 4-8 week production delay on single-source German supplier failure; Issue 3 names 8-12 week typical lead time. The plan's split-production schedule has no slack for either.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Production halts for weeks; pre-order chargebacks per Risk 9 trigger; funding trigger missed.

unmodelled_gates.label: European utility model for the gasket-channel manufacturing process filed before Month 6
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 13 names 6-month reverse-engineering window; without filing the design can be copied by Chinese manufacturers undercutting by 50%, eroding both Year-1 and B2B pivot margins.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Price erosion 50%; gross-profit margin collapses; pivot trigger and Year-1 net cash both miss.
